title: Catasetum dodsonianum
taxonomy: Catasetum dodsonianum is a species of orchid in the genus Catasetum. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This species is further categorized under the subfamily Catasetoideae and tribe Catasetinae. Catasetum dodsonianum is native to Brazil and is often found in the tropical regions of the country. The classification of Catasetum dodsonianum reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history as a member of the orchid family.

intro: Catasetum dodsonianum, also known as the Dodson's Catasetum, is an orchid species native to South America, characterized by its unique and vibrant flower structure. Traditionally, the plant is used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its potential antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Catasetum dodsonianum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its therapeutic effects.
